text.title: Instagram Profile, Reels, and Hashtag Strategy
text.summary: Profile optimization rules, Reels-first format priorities, Stories strategy, and hashtag minimalism rules.

rule.statement: Structure the Instagram bio as: line 1 = what you do for whom; line 2 = credibility/proof; line 3 = CTA.
rule.rationale: Visitors decide to follow in under 3 seconds. A three-line bio that answers "what is this account, why should I trust it, and what do I do next" converts profile visits to followers more reliably than a clever but ambiguous bio.

list.item: Display name: include a keyword (e.g., "Mike | SaaS Growth").
Profile photo: face clearly visible, bright, consistent with other platforms.
Highlights: About, Products/Services, Testimonials, FAQ, Free Resources.

rule.statement: Allocate 80% of content effort to Reels; write the hook separately and only ship Reels with hook score 4+/5.
rule.rationale: Reels deliver 2x the reach of photos by pushing to non-followers. The first 1.5 seconds determine algorithmic reach — a weak hook wastes the production effort. Rating hooks explicitly forces quality discipline.

rule.statement: Always include on-screen text in Reels; never rely on voiceover-only scripts.
rule.rationale: 80% of Instagram viewers watch muted. Scripts that put all content in voiceover fail to communicate to the majority of viewers.

list.item: Optimal Reels length: 7-15 seconds for virality; 30-60 seconds for educational depth.

rule.statement: Post Stories daily; pair every Reel with a Story re-share within 24 hours.
rule.rationale: Stories maintain relationship with existing followers while Reels reach new ones. Algorithm rewards accounts that use multiple formats consistently.

rule.statement: Use 3-5 hashtags per post (mixed small/medium/large tiers). Never request 30 hashtags from an agent.
rule.rationale: Meta now favors topic understanding over hashtag density. Over-indexing on hashtags underperforms and can look spammy. Agent prompts that request 30 hashtags produce stale, low-quality tag lists.

antipattern.reason: Instagram suppresses reach for Reels that contain the TikTok watermark. Cross-posted content must have the watermark stripped and ideally be re-edited for Instagram's format.
